channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump interviewing candidates for FBI chief, Vance says in later-deleted social media post
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is interviewing candidates for the role of FBI director, incoming Vice President JD Vance said Tuesday in the clearest indication yet that the new administration is looking to replace current director Christopher Wray.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Dr. Mehmet Oz, Trump's pick to lead Medicare and Medicaid
item.description: Mehmet Oz, a celebrity heart surgeon turned talk show host and lifestyle guru, is President-elect Donald Trump’s pick to run the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, the massive federal health care bureaucracy that covers more than a third of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Donald Trump to nominate Dr. Oz for Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump plans to nominate former Pennsylvania Senate nominee Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Administrator.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Trump Cabinet picks increase odds Edward Snowden could see life of freedom in the US
item.description: If Trump has his way, Tulsi Gabbard will be at the helm of U.S. intelligence and Matt Gaetz will be leading the DOJ, giving whistle-blower Edward Snowden his best chances yet at freedom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US will send Ukraine at least $275 million in new weapons in push to bolster Kyiv before Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will send Ukraine at least $275 million in new weapons, U.S. officials said Tuesday, as the Biden administration rushes to do as much as it can to help Kyiv fight back against Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Grassley: Gaetz Ethics report's release would result in 'faster consideration' of nomination
item.description: Sen. Chuck Grassley (Iowa), the incoming Republican chair of the Senate Judiciary Committee, said Tuesday that the House Ethics Committee would help speed up the Senate’s consideration of former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) to lead the Justice Department if it releases its report on Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems sue PA election board over uncounted provisional ballots amid Senate recount in Casey loss
item.description: The lawsuit by the DSCC centers on dozens of provisional ballots in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, that lacked a 'secrecy envelope'
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden Interior Dept puts together handbook to apply 'indigenous knowledge' into agency practices
item.description: Officials at the Department of the Interior are pushing to finalize a new "implementation handbook" to guide agency decision makers on how to "apply indigenous knowledge" in their day-to-day work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden appeals to world leaders to stay in the climate fight as Trump casts shadow
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — President Joe Biden made an urgent appeal to fellow world leaders Tuesday to surge money to developing nations to curb climate change as he wrapped up his final appearance at a major international summit.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Graham asks 51 intel officials on Hunter laptop letter if they'd still sign it now amid threats to clearances
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham sent a letter to each of the 51 former intelligence officials who signed a memo suggesting the Hunter Biden laptop had the hallmarks of Russian disinformation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump chooses TV's Dr. Oz to run Medicare and Medicaid, Wall Street exec Lutnick for Commerce
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he is nominating Dr. Mehmet Oz, who hosted a long-running television talk show, to lead the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Indigenous journalists question the accuracy of poll reported by some media organizations
item.description: An exit poll by Edison Research included a data point that 65 percent of American Indians supported Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Olson, MPR News

item.title: Federal workers brace for Trump overhaul of civil service
item.description: President-elect Trump’s plans to reshape the federal workforce would allow him to fire wide swaths of career employees and replace them with political appointees — a move that comes as he has demanded loyalty at every level of government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Businesses hope Trump administration knows value of migrants to US economy
item.description: As the Trump administration prepares to take back the White House, several business and migrant advocacy groups on Thursday said they hope the incoming administration realizes the economic contributions that migrants and asylum-seekers make to the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Controversial bathroom bill clears Ohio Senate, awaits Gov. DeWine's signature
item.description: The Ohio Senate voted 24-7 in favor of the ban, sending the measure to DeWine's desk for signature before it can become law.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, Fox 5

item.title: Trump picks Marco Rubio as his Secretary of State
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump named Sen. Marco Rubio of Florida as his nominee for secretary of state on Wednesday, setting up a onetime critic who evolved into one of the president-elect’s fiercest defenders to become the nation’s top diplomat.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
